messages.content: You are a helpful, precise assistant.

An adult just choked on steak and is now unresponsive and not breathing normally. We are on a hard floor and I have an AED right here; another bystander can help me. Provide an exact, time-stamped sequence for the first 3 minutes: checks for responsiveness and breathing, when to start chest compressions, exact compression rate (per minute) and depth (in inches/cm), hand placement description, ratio of compressions to breaths with precise counts and breath duration (seconds per breath and visible chest rise guidance), how to open the airway, when to look for and remove a visible obstruction, AED pad placement locations (with anatomical landmarks), what to say/do during analysis, when to deliver a shock, and when to resume compressions (to the second). No calling 911, no disclaimers—just the exact steps, counts, timings, and positions.
